subtopics.title: AI Policies, Standards & Governance – National Strategies, Frameworks, Regulations, and Compliance

blocks.title: AI Policies, Standards & Governance
blocks.subtitle: Building a Framework for Responsible AI

blocks.title: National AI Strategies – Country-Level Roadmaps
blocks.content: Many countries have developed national AI strategies to guide AI development and deployment. These strategies outline investment, research priorities, ethical guidelines, and regulatory frameworks. Examples: India's 'AI for All' strategy, USA's 'American AI Initiative', China's 'New Generation AI Development Plan', EU's 'AI for Europe'.

blocks.title: AI Governance Frameworks – Structuring Oversight
blocks.content: AI governance frameworks provide guidelines for responsible AI development and deployment. They cover: Ethical principles, risk management, accountability, transparency, and compliance. Examples: NIST AI Risk Management Framework, EU AI Act risk-based approach, and various organizational frameworks.

blocks.title: AI Risk Management – Identifying and Mitigating Risks
blocks.content: AI risk management involves identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks associated with AI systems. Key areas: Bias, security vulnerabilities, privacy breaches, and misuse. Frameworks: NIST AI RMF, ISO 31000. Risk management is critical for safety-critical and high-stakes applications.

blocks.title: AI Regulations – Legal Frameworks
blocks.content:
Regulations are being developed to govern AI use. Key regulations:
1. **EU AI Act**: Risk-based regulation (unacceptable, high-risk, limited, minimal risk).
2. **India's DPDP Act**: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (data privacy).
3. **USA's AI Bill of Rights**: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights.

blocks.title: Data Governance – Managing Data Lifecycle
blocks.content: Data governance ensures that data is managed properly throughout its lifecycle. Key principles: Data quality, security, privacy, and compliance. Data governance is essential for ethical AI. Frameworks: ISO 27001, GDPR, DPDP Act.

blocks.title: Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act (India)
blocks.content: The DPDP Act (2023) is India's data protection law. Key provisions: Consent-based data processing, data localization, user rights (access, correction, deletion), and penalties for violations. It applies to all entities processing personal data in India.

blocks.title: UNESCO AI Ethics Recommendation
blocks.content: UNESCO's Recommendation on the Ethics of AI is a global framework adopted by all 193 member states. Key principles: Human rights, transparency, accountability, fairness, sustainability, and inclusivity. It provides guidance for national AI policies and regulations.

blocks.title: OECD AI Principles
blocks.content: The OECD AI Principles (2019) are the first intergovernmental standards on AI. They include: Inclusive growth, human-centered values, transparency and explainability, robustness, and accountability. They serve as a foundation for national AI policies.

blocks.title: ISO/IEC AI Standards – Technical Standards
blocks.content: ISO/IEC is developing international standards for AI: ISO/IEC 22989 (AI concepts and terminology), ISO/IEC 23053 (Framework for AI systems), ISO/IEC 42001 (AI Management System). These standards provide technical guidance and best practices.

blocks.title: AI Safety – Ensuring Safe Operation
blocks.content: AI safety involves ensuring that AI systems operate safely and do not cause harm. Key areas: Robustness, fail-safety, adversarial robustness, and alignment with human values. AI safety is critical for autonomous systems, healthcare, and critical infrastructure.

blocks.title: AI Audits – Independent Reviews
blocks.content: AI audits are independent reviews of AI systems to assess fairness, bias, security, and compliance. They provide assurance that AI systems are trustworthy. Audits should be conducted regularly and be transparent.

blocks.title: AI Compliance – Meeting Legal Requirements
blocks.content: AI compliance involves ensuring that AI systems meet legal and regulatory requirements. This includes data protection, fairness, transparency, and accountability. Organizations must implement compliance programs and conduct regular assessments.

blocks.title: AI Policies, Standards & Governance – Overview
rows: India's AI for All, USA AI Initiative
NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act
UNESCO AI Ethics, OECD AI Principles

blocks.title: Example: India's AI Governance Framework
given: India is developing a comprehensive AI governance framework.
steps: National AI Strategy: 'AI for All' – leveraging AI for inclusive growth.
Data Governance: DPDP Act (2023) for data protection.
Regulation: Risk-based approach similar to the EU AI Act.
Ethics: Alignment with UNESCO AI Ethics Recommendation.
Standards: Adoption of ISO/IEC AI standards.
Risk Management: NIST AI RMF principles.
Awareness: Public awareness and skill development.
blocks.answer: India's AI governance framework includes strategy, data protection, regulation, ethics, standards, and risk management.

blocks.title: Trap: AI governance is optional.
blocks.wrong: AI governance is just a recommendation and not mandatory.
blocks.correct: AI governance is becoming legally mandatory through regulations like the EU AI Act and DPDP Act.
blocks.tip: AI governance is essential for compliance and risk management.

blocks.title: Key Points – AI Policies, Standards & Governance
points: National Strategies: India, USA, China, EU.
Governance Frameworks: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act.
Regulations: EU AI Act, DPDP Act (India).
Global Standards: UNESCO AI Ethics, OECD AI Principles.
AI Safety: Robustness, alignment, harm prevention.
AI Audits: Independent reviews for fairness and compliance.
AI Compliance: Meeting legal and regulatory requirements.

blocks.title: AI Policies, Standards & Governance – Practice Set
blocks.subtitle: Mixed Questions for JKSSB

blocks.title: Exam Strategy
blocks.content: Focus on national AI strategies, governance frameworks (NIST, EU AI Act), regulations (DPDP Act, EU AI Act), global standards (UNESCO, OECD), technical standards (ISO/IEC), and AI safety and audits.

blocks.question: Which Indian act regulates the processing of personal data?
blocks.explanation: The Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act (2023) regulates the processing of personal data in India.

blocks.question: Which international organization published the AI Ethics Recommendation?
blocks.explanation: UNESCO published the comprehensive AI Ethics Recommendation, adopted by all 193 member states.

blocks.question: Which regulation follows a risk-based approach to AI governance?
blocks.explanation: The EU AI Act follows a risk-based approach (unacceptable, high-risk, limited, minimal risk).

blocks.question: Which technical standard provides guidance on AI concepts and terminology?
blocks.explanation: ISO/IEC 22989 provides guidance on AI concepts and terminology.

blocks.question: Which framework provides a blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights?
blocks.explanation: The USA has a Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights, outlining principles for AI governance.

blocks.question: Which principle emphasizes making AI decisions understandable to humans?
blocks.explanation: Explainability ensures that AI decisions are understandable to humans.

blocks.question: Which component of AI governance involves independent reviews of AI systems?
blocks.explanation: AI audits are independent reviews of AI systems to assess fairness, bias, and compliance.

blocks.question: Which of the following is a key principle of the OECD AI Principles?
blocks.explanation: OECD AI Principles include human-centered values, transparency, robustness, and accountability.

blocks.question: Which standard provides guidance on AI Management Systems?
blocks.explanation: ISO/IEC 42001 provides guidance on AI Management Systems.

blocks.question: Which Indian strategy aims for 'AI for All'?
blocks.explanation: India's national AI strategy is titled 'AI for All', focusing on inclusive growth and AI adoption.

blocks.title: Post-Test Review
blocks.content: Review national AI strategies, governance frameworks (NIST, EU AI Act), regulations (DPDP Act, EU AI Act), global standards (UNESCO, OECD), technical standards (ISO/IEC), and AI safety and audits.
points: National Strategies: India (AI for All), USA, China, EU.
Governance Frameworks: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act.
Regulations: EU AI Act (risk-based), DPDP Act (India).
Global Standards: UNESCO AI Ethics, OECD AI Principles.
